action.reasoning: Observation: eval is stuck at 0.1623 while local best_miou printed by train is 0.2479, and the last iteration spent ~90 minutes mostly on training plus ~24 minutes local prediction. Analysis: the gap strongly suggests the current training objective/validation proxy is still mismatched to full-resolution eval, and another long SegFormer-B5 continuation is unlikely to yield a large jump within the remaining budget. ADE20K is well served by MMSeg baselines; using the preinstalled mmsegmentation stack with a strong pretrained backbone and standard decode head is the highest-confidence path now. To respect time, I will switch to a standard UPerNet+MiT-B5 config, train on 4 GPUs with a reduced schedule and proper val, and keep our existing predict.py/checkpoint path untouched for now while measuring the new training speed/quality. If this run produces a clearly better val checkpoint, next iteration I can either adapt predict.py to load the mmseg checkpoint or export weights. Decision: add an MMSeg training script that builds ADE20K datasets from /data, trains UPerNet-MiT-B5 with AMP on 4 GPUs for a short but meaningful schedule, and reports val mIoU. No eval submission this iteration because predict.py cannot yet consume the new checkpoint format.
